headlines.title: Saudi Arabia Expected to Slash Oil Prices Again
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia is expected to slash again the official selling prices of the crude it will load for Asia in July amid weakening demand and narrowing spot Middle East crude premiums, a Reuters survey of industry sources showed on Friday. headlines.title: China’s Export Prices Jump as Oil Shock Hits Factory Costs
headlines.summary: China’s export prices saw their biggest gain in three years in April as the oil price surge filtered through the goods manufactured in the world’s biggest exporter of goods. headlines.title: U.S. Extends Deadline for Sale of Lukoil’s Global Assets Again
headlines.summary: The U.S. federal government has extended the deadline for talks between Lukoil and potential buyers of its foreign business by another month, until June 27, suggesting the forced divestment process is turning out to be more complicated than maybe hoped for. headlines.title: Mitsui Eyes Global LNG Deals to Power the AI Data Center Boom
